title: ranunculus acris subsp. vulgatus
intro: Ranunculus acris subsp. vulgatus, also known as the meadow buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus acris plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the uterus and relieve menstrual cramps, due to its rich content of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Ranunculus acris subsp. vulgatus include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which make it useful in treating various conditions such as menstrual disorders, respiratory issues, and skin problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and its roots and leaves are commonly used in traditional medicine.
